sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports a history of depression and has been using medication and attending therapy weekly. She reports sleeping well at night and denies thoughts of self-harm. She reports headaches that she attributes to looking at a computer screen all day. She reports that her carpal tunnel release has been doing well.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam reveals clear lungs, normal heart sounds, equal grips, and intact sensation. Blood pressure is slightly elevated in the office today. Labs are within normal limits.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient has hypertension and depression. The carpal tunnel release is stable.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Increase lisinopril to 10 mg daily. Schedule a follow-up appointment in one month to assess blood pressure control. Continue current medication regimen for depression and encourage continued therapy attendance. Advise patient to report any recurrence of carpal tunnel symptoms.
